code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a comics character (or related entity)
    into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    # Relative phrase builders (omit subject, used when multiple predicates share the same subject)
    relative_builders = {
        "alternativeName": lambda o: f"is also known as {o}",
        "creator": lambda o: f"was created by {o}",
        "fullName": lambda o: f"has full name {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda o: f"was founded by {o}",
        "keyPerson": lambda o: f"has key person {o}",
        "city": lambda o: f"is located in {o}",
        "product": lambda o: f"produces {o}",
        "lastAired": lambda o: f"last episode aired on {o}",
        "starring": lambda o: f"stars {o}",
        "firstAppearanceInFilm": lambda o: f"first appeared in the film {o}",
        "voice": lambda o: f"has voice provided by {o}",
        "distributor": lambda o: f"was distributed by {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda o: f"was born in {o}",
        "nationality": lambda o: f"is from {o}",
        "award": lambda o: f"received the {o}",
        "broadcastedBy": lambda o: f"was broadcasted by {o}",
        "firstAired": lambda o: f"first aired on {o}",
        "series": lambda o: f"is part of the series {o}",
        "child": lambda o: f"is a child of {o}",
    }

    # Group objects by (subject, predicate) for aggregation
    grouped = {}
    for triple in triples:
        key = (triple.subject, triple.predicate)
        grouped.setdefault(key, []).append(triple.object)

    # Helper to join multiple objects nicely
    def join_items(items: list[str]) -> str:
        if not items:
            return ""
        if len(items) == 1:
            return items[0]
        return ", ".join(items[:-1]) + " and " + items[-1]

    # Organize by subject to build a single clause per subject
    subject_map = {}
    for (subject, predicate), objects in grouped.items():
        subject_map.setdefault(subject, []).append((predicate, objects))

    clauses = []
    for subject, pred_objs in subject_map.items():
        fragments = []
        for predicate, objects in pred_objs:
            rel_builder = relative_builders.get(predicate)
            obj_str = join_items(objects)
            if rel_builder:
                fragments.append(rel_builder(obj_str))
            else:
                fragments.append(f"{predicate} {obj_str}")
        # Combine fragments into a coherent clause
        if len(fragments) == 1:
            clause = f"{subject} {fragments[0]}"
        else:
            clause = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(fragments[:-1]) + ", and " + fragments[-1]
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Assemble final sentence
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        return clauses[0] + "."
    return ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
